extracted_codes.context: For which there is an ICD-9-CM / ICD-10-CM diagnosis of prostate cancer
For which there is an ICD-9-CM / ICD-10-CM diagnosis of prostate cancer
For which there is either prostatectomy surgery or radiation procedure performed as identified by CPT codes

clinical_criteria: Male patients of 35 years of age or more
At least two encounters before first treatment
At least one clinical notes before first treatment
Either prostatectomy surgery or radiation procedure performed
UI status extracted at baseline and after treatment (3months, 6months, 12months and 24 months)
algorithm_summary: A weakly supervised machine learning approach that extracts urinary incontinence status from clinical narratives for prostate cancer patients. The algorithm uses weighted neural word embeddings with TF-idf scoring and domain-specific dictionaries combined with CLEVER terminology for weak supervision. Sentence-level vectors are used to train a model that classifies notes as: (1) Affirmed - symptom present, (2) Negated - symptom negated, (3) Discussed Risk - risk discussion documented, or (4) Not discussed. Final note-level labels are assigned using majority voting of sentence-level annotations. The cohort includes male patients ≥35 years with prostate cancer diagnosis who underwent prostatectomy or radiation therapy.
